---
title: "Final Treasury Yield Forecasting Project: Univariate and Multivariate Time-Series Analysis"
author: "Jackson Wang"
date: "2026-06-12"
output: pdf_document
---

# Introduction

This report develops forecasting models for monthly U.S. Treasury yields using the 1-year, 2-year, 3-year, and 5-year constant maturity series from FRED. The data run from June 1976 through May 2026.

The goal is to compare several univariate and multivariate time-series models for forecasting Treasury yield movements. The report estimates AR(1), AR(p), ETS(ANN), VAR, and VARIMA-style models. Model fit is compared using AICc and BIC, and the final results include four-month forecasts.

# Reproducible script

The full cleaned R script is available at:

```text
scripts/part2_forecasting_models.R
```

# Background

Part 1 shows that the original Treasury yield series have long-term interest-rate cycles and are not stationary. Therefore, the models in Part 2 use first differences:

- d_GS1
- d_GS2
- d_GS3
- d_GS5

# Models

## AR(1)

AR(1) models are estimated separately for each transformed yield series. The AR(1) terms are statistically significant, while the constants are not significant.

## AR(p)

AR(p) models are selected using BIC across lag orders 1 through 12.

Selected lag orders:

| Maturity | Selected p |
|---|---:|
| d_GS1 | 6 |
| d_GS2 | 6 |
| d_GS3 | 2 |
| d_GS5 | 2 |

## ETS(ANN)

ETS(ANN) models are included as benchmark exponential smoothing models.

## VAR

VAR models are included to capture cross-maturity relationships among Treasury yield changes.

# Conclusion

The models generally forecast small monthly changes near zero, consistent with the transformed series having near-zero long-run average changes. AR(p) models improve on AR(1), and VAR models are useful because Treasury yield changes across maturities move together.
